name: Deficit Push-Up
instructions: Place two stable, equal-height blocks (books, low stools) roughly shoulder-width apart.
Grip each block and set up in a push-up position with your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest below hand level, going deeper than a standard push-up allows.
Press back up to the starting position, keeping elbows at roughly 45 degrees.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: Push-up performed with hands elevated on blocks or books to increase range of motion.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Extra depth below hand level increases chest stretch under load.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your core braced throughout.
Lower under control, don't drop.
Full lockout at the top.

name: Archer Push-Up
instructions: Set up in a wide push-up position, hands well outside shoulder width.
Bend one elbow to lower your chest toward that hand, keeping the other arm straight and extending out to the side.
Press back up to the starting position, alternating sides each repetition.
Keep hips and shoulders square to the floor throughout.
movementPatternDescription: Push-up variation that shifts weight onto one arm while the other stays extended, building toward one-arm push-ups.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: One-sided loading demands significant shoulder and core stability.
coachingCues: Keep the straight arm engaged, not just resting.
Move slowly on the way down.
Keep hips level, avoid rotating.

name: Wide Grip Push-Up
instructions: Set up in a push-up position with hands roughly 1.5x shoulder width apart.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor with elbows flaring slightly.
Press back up to full extension.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: Push-up with hands set wider than shoulder width to bias the chest over the triceps.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep core braced.
Don't let hips sag.
Control the descent.
commonMistakes: Hands too wide causing shoulder strain

name: Hindu Push-Up
instructions: Start in a downward-dog position: hips high, hands and feet on the floor, forming an inverted V.
Bend your elbows and dive your chest forward and down in an arc, sweeping close to the floor.
Continue the arc until your hips lower and your chest rises, ending in an upward-dog position with arms extended.
Reverse the motion back to the starting downward-dog position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A flowing push-up variation that moves from downward-dog into a cobra-like upward-dog position, combining pushing strength with shoulder mobility.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Full-body flowing arc through a large range of motion.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the motion fluid, not choppy.
Let your hips lead the transition.
Avoid shrugging your shoulders at the top.
commonMistakes: Rushing the arc and skipping range
Collapsing the lower back at the top

name: Elevated Pike Push-Up
instructions: Place your feet on a stable chair or step, hands on the floor, forming an inverted V shape.
Walk hands back and hips up until your torso is close to vertical.
Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: Pike push-up performed with feet raised on a chair or step to increase vertical loading on the shoulders.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep elbows tracking forward, not flared.
Look slightly forward, not straight down.
Use a stable, non-slip surface.

name: Box Pike Push-Up
instructions: Place your hands shoulder-width apart on a sturdy box or step.
Walk your feet in to form an inverted V, hips high.
Lower the crown of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Press back up until your arms are extended.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: Pike push-up with hands elevated on a box or step, deepening the range of motion for the shoulders.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the box stable and non-slip.
Control the descent rather than dropping.

name: Wall Walk
instructions: Start in a push-up position with feet against the base of a wall.
Walk your feet up the wall while walking your hands closer to the wall, keeping your body as straight as possible.
Continue until your body is close to vertical, chest facing the wall.
Reverse the movement, walking hands out and feet back down.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: An advanced bodyweight drill that builds toward a handstand by walking the feet up a wall while walking the hands in.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires significant shoulder, wrist, and core stability approaching an inverted position.
coachingCues: Keep small, controlled steps.
Brace your core the entire time.
Stop before losing tight form.
commonMistakes: Walking too fast and losing control

name: Handstand Hold
instructions: Kick up into a handstand against a wall, or walk into position using the Wall Walk.
Keep your body in a straight, tight line with your core and glutes braced.
Hold the position for the target duration, breathing steadily.
Lower down with control, or walk back down the wall.
movementPatternDescription: An isometric hold in an inverted handstand position against a wall, building shoulder strength and body control.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Isometric — minimal joint movement, focus on maintaining a straight, rigid line.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Push the floor away through your hands.
Squeeze glutes and brace your core.
Keep breathing, don't hold your breath.

name: Incline Pike Push-Up
instructions: Place your hands on a stable elevated surface such as a step or bench.
Walk your feet back to form an inverted V with hips high.
Bend your elbows to lower your head toward your hands.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: An easier pike push-up variation with hands elevated on a bench or step, reducing the load for beginners.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips high throughout.
Move under control.
commonMistakes: Letting hips drop toward the floor

name: Feet Elevated Archer Push-Up
instructions: Place your feet on a stable step or box and hands wide on the floor.
Shift your weight to one arm, bending that elbow while keeping the other arm extended out to the side.
Press back up to center, then alternate sides.
Keep hips level throughout the movement.
movementPatternDescription: An advanced archer push-up with feet raised on a step, increasing upper-chest and shoulder demand.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the straight arm actively engaged.
Avoid letting hips rotate.

name: Sliding Chest Fly
instructions: Kneel or lie face-down with hands on sliders (or a folded towel on a smooth floor) directly under your shoulders.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, slide both hands out to the sides as you lower your chest toward the floor.
Feel a stretch across the chest at the bottom of the movement.
Squeeze your chest to slide your hands back in and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A floor-based fly using sliders (or towels on a smooth floor) to mimic a dumbbell fly pattern using only bodyweight.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout.
Control the slide out, don't let it collapse.
Squeeze the chest to bring hands back in.
commonMistakes: Bending elbows too much, turning it into a press
Sliding surface too rough or too slippery to control

name: Ring Fly
instructions: Set rings or straps to roughly chest height and grip a handle in each hand, leaning forward with arms extended.
Keeping a slight bend in the elbows, open your arms out to the sides as you lower your chest between the handles.
Feel a deep stretch across the chest at the bottom.
Squeeze your chest to bring your arms back together and return to the start.
Adjust your foot position closer to or further from the anchor point to change difficulty.
movementPatternDescription: A chest fly performed on gymnastics rings or suspension straps, if available, offering adjustable resistance via body angle.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires gymnastics rings or suspension straps.
coachingCues: Keep your core braced throughout.
Control the eccentric, don't let arms snap open.
commonMistakes: Leaning too far forward too soon
Losing tension in the straight-arm position

name: Push-Up Hold
instructions: Lower into the bottom position of a push-up, chest a few inches from the floor.
Hold this position, keeping your body in a straight line and core braced.
Maintain steady breathing throughout the hold.
Press back up to the top once the hold is complete.
movementPatternDescription: An isometric hold at the bottom of a push-up, building time-under-tension strength through the chest and triceps.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Isometric — no movement, focus on maintaining position under tension.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips in line with shoulders.
Don't let the chest touch the floor.
Breathe steadily throughout.

name: Push-Up Pulses
instructions: Lower into the bottom quarter of a push-up.
Pulse up and down a few inches, staying in the lower range without locking out.
Keep tension on the chest throughout the set.
Continue for the recommended repetitions before resetting.
movementPatternDescription: Small, controlled pulsing reps performed in the bottom half of the push-up range to maximize chest time-under-tension.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Small partial-range pulses to maximize time under tension.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the range small and controlled.
Don't let hips sag.
Keep constant tension, avoid resting at the top.
commonMistakes: Pulsing too high into the range, losing tension

name: Pike Lean Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand side-on to a stable wall or door frame and lean your body away from it, gripping the frame with one hand at hip height.
Lean your bodyweight away, letting the working shoulder support the lean.
Slowly raise your torso back to vertical using the working shoulder, similar to a lateral raise arc.
Control the return to the leaning position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions, then switch sides.
movementPatternDescription: A leaning bodyweight lateral raise substitute performed by shifting weight onto one arm to load the opposite side delt through leaning.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Partial arc controlled by body lean angle.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the working arm mostly straight.
Control the lean, don't swing.
Keep shoulders level, avoid shrugging.
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of shoulder control
Gripping too high or too low on the frame

name: Side Plank Shoulder Raise
instructions: Set up in a side plank, forearm or hand on the floor, body in a straight line.
Raise your top arm straight up toward the ceiling, then out to shoulder height, keeping the elbow straight.
Lower the arm back down with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions, then switch sides.
movementPatternDescription: A side plank with an added top-arm raise, combining core stability with a bodyweight lateral raise pattern.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips stacked and lifted.
Move the arm slowly, avoid swinging.
Keep the core braced throughout.

name: Isometric Lateral Hold
instructions: Stand tall with arms relaxed at your sides.
Raise both arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, palms facing down.
Hold this position, keeping shoulders down and away from your ears.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, then lower with control.
movementPatternDescription: A static hold with arms raised to shoulder height, building side delt endurance and control without equipment.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep shoulders down, not shrugged.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Breathe steadily throughout.

name: Reverse Snow Angel
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with arms extended overhead, palms down.
Lift your chest and arms slightly off the floor.
Sweep your arms out and down toward your hips in a wide arc, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Reverse the arc back to the starting overhead position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A prone floor exercise that sweeps the arms in an arc like a snow angel, targeting the rear delts and upper back.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Squeeze shoulder blades together at the midpoint.
Keep the movement slow and controlled.
Keep neck neutral, don't crane upward.
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the arms

name: Prone Y Raise
instructions: Lie face down with arms extended overhead in a Y shape, thumbs pointing up.
Lift your arms and chest slightly off the floor, leading with your thumbs.
Squeeze your shoulder blades down and together at the top.
Lower back down with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A prone raise with arms forming a Y overhead, targeting the lower traps and rear delts.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Lead with the thumbs, not the elbows.
Keep the range small and controlled.
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up.

name: Superman Pull
instructions: Lie face down with arms extended overhead.
Lift your chest and arms slightly off the floor.
Pull your elbows back and down as if rowing, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Extend arms back overhead to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A prone row-like pulling motion performed against bodyweight, targeting the rear delts and upper back.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Squeeze shoulder blades at the top of the pull.
Keep the neck neutral.
Move slowly and with control.
commonMistakes: Yanking the arms back too fast

name: Reverse Plank
instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended and hands placed behind your hips, fingers pointing toward your feet.
Press through your hands and heels to lift your hips off the floor into a straight line from shoulders to ankles.
Hold the position, keeping your core and glutes braced.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, then lower with control.
movementPatternDescription: A tabletop-style isometric hold facing upward, building rear delt and posterior chain endurance.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips lifted, don't let them sag.
Press the floor away through your hands.
Keep neck neutral, don't drop the head back.

name: Bodyweight Triceps Extension
instructions: Stand facing a wall, arms extended overhead with palms flat against the wall above your head.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, bend your elbows to lower your head toward the wall.
Press back through your palms, extending your elbows to return to the start.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A wall-assisted triceps extension using bodyweight lean to load the triceps without equipment.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep upper arms still, only the elbows move.
Control the lean toward the wall.
commonMistakes: Letting the upper arms drift forward

name: Wall Triceps Extension
instructions: Stand an arm's length from a wall, hands placed on the wall at chest height, close together.
Keep your body in a straight line and bend your elbows to bring your chest toward the wall.
Press back through your palms to extend your elbows fully.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A close-stance wall press variation isolating the triceps through a short range of elbow extension.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep elbows tracking backward, close to the body.
Keep core braced throughout.
commonMistakes: Hands placed too wide, shifting focus to chest

name: Isometric Lockout Hold
instructions: Set up in a high plank / top-of-push-up position with arms fully extended.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Hold the locked-out position, actively pressing the floor away.
Maintain the hold for the target duration.
movementPatternDescription: A static top-of-lockout hold from a push-up position, building triceps endurance without any equipment.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Actively push the floor away, don't just rest in the position.
Keep hips level with shoulders.
commonMistakes: Locking out passively instead of pressing actively

name: Towel Row
instructions: Loop a sturdy towel around a secure anchor point such as a solid door frame edge or pole, holding one end in each hand.
Walk your feet back and lean your body backward, arms extended, keeping the towel taut.
Pull your chest toward the anchor by driving your elbows back, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Extend your arms back out with control to the starting lean position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions. Increase difficulty by leaning back further.
movementPatternDescription: A doorway row using a towel looped around a sturdy anchor (door frame or pole) to mimic a cable row using only bodyweight.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a sturdy towel and a secure anchor point such as a solid door frame.
coachingCues: Keep your body in a straight line, don't bend at the hips.
Drive elbows back, not out to the sides.
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top of the pull.
commonMistakes: Bending at the hips instead of staying rigid
Using a door that can swing open

name: Bed Sheet Row
instructions: Loop a long, sturdy bed sheet around a secure anchor point, holding one end in each hand.
Lean back with arms extended and feet planted, keeping the sheet taut.
Pull your chest toward the anchor point by driving your elbows back.
Extend your arms back out with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A row substitute using a long bed sheet or strap anchored to a stable point, following the same pattern as a towel row.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a sturdy sheet or strap and a secure anchor point.
coachingCues: Keep your body rigid, hinge only at the ankles.
Pull with your back, not just your arms.
commonMistakes: Using a stretchy or weak fabric that can slip or tear

name: Superman Row
instructions: Lie face down with arms extended in front of you.
Lift your chest and arms slightly off the floor.
Pull your elbows back and down in a rowing motion, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Extend your arms back out to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A prone rowing motion performed against bodyweight resistance, useful as a no-equipment fallback when no anchor point is available.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
Keep the neck neutral throughout.
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of controlled pulling

name: Y Raise Hold
instructions: Lie face down with arms extended overhead in a Y shape, thumbs up.
Lift your arms and chest slightly off the floor.
Hold this raised position, squeezing your shoulder blades down and together.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, then lower with control.
movementPatternDescription: A static hold in the Y-raise position, building lower trap endurance without equipment.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the neck neutral.
Lead with the thumbs, not the elbows.
commonMistakes: Shrugging shoulders up toward the ears

name: Dead Hang Shrug
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed.
Without bending your elbows, pull your shoulder blades down and together, raising your body slightly.
Hold briefly at the top of the shrug.
Relax back down to a full dead hang with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A shrugging motion performed while hanging from a pull-up bar, isolating the traps and building grip and shoulder health.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep elbows straight throughout — this is a shrug, not a pull-up.
Initiate the movement from the shoulder blades.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a pull-up

name: Commando Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with one hand in front of the other, both palms facing each other.
Pull your body up so your head passes to one side of the bar.
Lower back down with control to a full hang.
Repeat, alternating which side your head passes on each repetition.
movementPatternDescription: A pull-up variation gripping the bar with one hand in front of the other, alternating sides to pull the head to each side of the bar.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the pull controlled, avoid swinging.
Alternate sides evenly.
Lower to a full hang each rep.
commonMistakes: Not fully extending arms at the bottom

name: Jumping Pull-Up
instructions: Stand under a pull-up bar you can jump to reach, or use a low bar.
Grip the bar and use a small jump to help drive your chin above the bar.
Lower yourself down slowly and with control, resisting the descent.
Reset your feet and repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: An assisted pull-up variation using a jump to help drive the body upward, useful for building toward strict pull-ups.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a pull-up bar (low enough to jump to, or a step to jump from).
coachingCues: Control the lowering phase — that's where most of the benefit comes from.
Use just enough jump assistance to get your chin over.
commonMistakes: Relying entirely on the jump with no pulling effort
Dropping quickly instead of lowering under control

name: Neutral Grip Pull-Up
instructions: Grip parallel neutral-grip handles on a pull-up bar, palms facing each other.
Hang with arms fully extended.
Pull yourself up until your chin clears the handles, keeping elbows close to your body.
Lower back down with control to a full hang.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A pull-up performed with palms facing each other on parallel handles, often more shoulder-friendly than a pronated grip.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a pull-up bar with neutral-grip handles.
coachingCues: Keep elbows tracking close to your torso.
Avoid swinging or kipping.
Fully extend arms at the bottom.

name: Towel Curl
instructions: Stand on one end of a towel with one foot, holding the other end in the same-side hand.
Keeping tension on the towel, curl your hand up toward your shoulder, resisting with your foot.
Lower back down with control, maintaining constant tension.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions, then switch sides.
movementPatternDescription: A self-resisted bicep curl using a towel under one foot to create tension through the curling arm.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep constant tension on the towel throughout.
Curl using your bicep, not your shoulder.
commonMistakes: Letting the towel go slack between reps

name: Isometric Towel Curl
instructions: Hold one end of a towel in each hand, elbow of the working arm bent to about 90 degrees.
Use your other hand to pull down on the towel while your working arm curls upward against that resistance.
Hold the contraction at a fixed point for the target duration.
Switch sides and repeat.
movementPatternDescription: A static bicep contraction using a towel pulled against your own opposing hand for resistance.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep tension constant and even.
Keep the working elbow tucked at your side.

name: Door Frame Curl
instructions: Stand beside an open door frame and grip the inside edge with one hand, elbow bent around 90 degrees.
Pull upward and inward against the fixed frame as if curling, engaging the bicep.
Hold the contraction for the target duration.
Switch sides and repeat.
movementPatternDescription: An isometric bicep curl performed by pulling against a fixed door frame, useful when no other equipment is available.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep the elbow tucked at your side.
Pull with steady, even force, don't jerk.
commonMistakes: Using an unstable or hollow door frame
Jerking instead of a steady pull

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand a couple of feet in front of a chair or step, and rest the top of one foot on it behind you.
Keep your torso upright and lower your hips until your front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
movementPatternDescription: A single-leg squat with the rear foot elevated behind you, building unilateral leg strength using only bodyweight.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep most of your weight on the front leg.
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes.
Keep your torso tall throughout.
commonMistakes: Placing too much weight through the rear foot
Letting the front knee cave inward

name: Single Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand on one leg with a soft bend in the knee.
Hinge forward at the hips, extending your free leg straight back behind you for counterbalance.
Lower your torso until it's roughly parallel to the floor, keeping your back flat.
Squeeze your glute and hamstring to return to standing.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
movementPatternDescription: A single-leg hip hinge that builds hamstring and glute strength while challenging balance, using only bodyweight.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your hips square to the floor.
Hinge at the hips, don't just bend forward at the waist.
Keep a soft bend in the standing knee throughout.

name: Hip Hinge
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, a soft bend in the knees.
Push your hips straight back while keeping your shins nearly vertical, letting your torso lean forward.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your back flat.
Drive your hips forward to return to standing, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A foundational bodyweight hip-hinge drill that teaches and reinforces proper hinge mechanics for hamstring and glute development.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Push the hips back first, not down.
Keep a neutral spine throughout.
Squeeze glutes at the top.
commonMistakes: Squatting down instead of hinging back

name: Spanish Squat
instructions: Loop a sturdy band or strap around a fixed anchor at roughly knee height, and step into the loop so it sits behind both knees.
Walk out until there's tension on the band, feet shoulder-width apart.
Sit back into a squat, using the band tension to stay upright as you lower.
Push through your feet to return to standing.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A squat variation using a band or strap anchored around a sturdy pole at knee height to allow a more upright torso, heavily emphasizing the quads.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a resistance band or strap and a sturdy low anchor point.
coachingCues: Keep your torso upright throughout.
Sit straight down, not back excessively.
Keep tension in the band the whole set.
commonMistakes: Leaning too far forward, losing the quad emphasis

name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and walk your feet out slightly.
Slide down the wall until your knees are bent to roughly 90 degrees, thighs parallel to the floor.
Hold the position, keeping your back flat against the wall.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, then slide back up to stand.
movementPatternDescription: An isometric squat hold with your back against a wall, building quad endurance with zero equipment.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your knees tracking over your ankles, not past your toes.
Press your lower back flat against the wall.
Breathe steadily throughout.
commonMistakes: Sliding too low or too high on the wall

name: Nordic Curl
instructions: Kneel on a cushioned surface with your feet anchored under something sturdy (a couch, or a partner holding your ankles).
Keeping your body in a straight line from knees to head, slowly lower your torso toward the floor by resisting with your hamstrings.
Lower as far as you can with control, catching yourself with your hands if needed.
Push back up to the starting kneeling position, using your hands to assist if necessary.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: An advanced eccentric hamstring exercise performed kneeling with the feet anchored, lowering the torso under control.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a sturdy anchor for your feet (heavy furniture, or a partner).
coachingCues: Lower as slowly as your hamstrings allow.
Keep your hips extended, don't fold at the waist.
Use your hands to catch yourself safely if you lose control.
commonMistakes: Bending at the hips instead of staying rigid

name: Sliding Hamstring Curl
instructions: Lie on your back with heels resting on sliders or a folded towel on a smooth floor, knees bent.
Lift your hips off the floor into a glute bridge position.
Keeping your hips up, slide your heels away from you to straighten your legs.
Pull your heels back in using your hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A supine leg curl using sliders or a towel on a smooth floor, targeting the hamstrings without equipment.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires sliders or a towel on a smooth floor.
coachingCues: Keep your hips lifted throughout the whole movement.
Move slowly and with control on the slide out.
commonMistakes: Letting hips drop during the slide
Sliding surface too rough to move smoothly

name: Glute Bridge Walkout
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips into a bridge position.
While holding the bridge, walk your feet out a few inches, then back in, without letting your hips drop.
Continue walking out and in for the recommended repetitions, then lower down.
movementPatternDescription: A glute bridge with an added leg walkout, extending time under tension for the glutes and hamstrings using only bodyweight.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips level and lifted throughout.
Squeeze glutes at the top of the bridge.
Take small, controlled steps.
commonMistakes: Letting hips sag during the walkout

name: Single Leg Calf Raise
instructions: Stand on one foot, holding onto a wall or chair for balance if needed.
Rise up onto the ball of your foot as high as possible.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing the calf.
Lower back down with control until your heel nearly touches the floor.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
movementPatternDescription: A unilateral bodyweight calf raise, doubling the load compared to a two-legged version.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Rise straight up, don't let your ankle roll.
Pause at the top for a full squeeze.
Lower all the way down for a full stretch.

name: Body Saw
instructions: Set up in a forearm plank with your toes on sliders, gliding discs, or a towel on a smooth floor.
Keeping your core braced and body in a straight line, push your body forward slightly by rolling onto your toes.
Pull your body back to the starting plank position using your abdominals.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A plank variation where the body slides forward and back (using sliders or on a smooth floor), building anti-extension core strength.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires sliders or a towel on a smooth floor.
coachingCues: Keep your hips level throughout, don't let them pike up or sag.
Move slowly and with control.
commonMistakes: Letting hips sag during the forward slide
Moving too far, losing plank position

name: Copenhagen Plank
instructions: Lie on your side and place your top foot on a chair or bench behind you, bottom leg free beneath it.
Prop yourself up on your bottom forearm and lift your hips into a side plank position.
Keep your body in a straight line, bracing through your core and inner thigh.
Hold the position for the target duration, then switch sides.
movementPatternDescription: A side plank variation with the top leg supported on a bench or chair, heavily loading the adductors and obliques.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
homeGymNotes: Requires a chair or bench to support the top leg.
coachingCues: Keep hips lifted and square.
Brace through the obliques and inner thigh.
Don't let hips rotate forward or back.
commonMistakes: Unstable chair or bench used for the top leg

name: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Extend one arm straight forward and the opposite leg straight back simultaneously.
Hold briefly, keeping your hips and shoulders square to the floor.
Return to the starting position with control and repeat on the other side.
movementPatternDescription: A quadruped anti-rotation exercise that extends the opposite arm and leg while maintaining a stable, level spine.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your hips level, don't let them rotate.
Move slowly, avoid rushing.
Keep your neck neutral, eyes toward the floor.
commonMistakes: Rotating the hips as the leg extends

name: Bear Crawl Hold
instructions: Start on all fours, hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Lift your knees an inch off the floor, keeping your back flat.
Hold this hovering position, keeping weight distributed evenly across hands and feet.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, breathing steadily throughout.
movementPatternDescription: A static hold in the bear crawl position, knees hovering just off the floor, building full-body core stability.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your back flat, not rounded or arched.
Keep knees low, just off the floor.
Distribute weight evenly through hands and feet.
commonMistakes: Raising hips too high, turning it into a downward dog

name: Hanging L-Sit
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms fully extended.
Keeping your legs straight, raise them until they're parallel to the floor, forming an L shape with your torso.
Hold the position, keeping your core braced and legs together.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, then lower your legs with control.
movementPatternDescription: An advanced hanging hold with legs raised to horizontal, forming an L shape, building serious core and hip flexor strength.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep legs straight and together.
Brace your core, don't just rely on hip flexors.
Keep shoulders down away from your ears.
commonMistakes: Bending the knees to fake the range

name: Toes To Bar
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms fully extended.
Engage your core and lats, and raise your legs, keeping them as straight as possible, until your toes touch the bar.
Lower your legs back down with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions, avoiding excessive swinging.
movementPatternDescription: An advanced hanging core exercise that raises the feet all the way to touch the pull-up bar.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Initiate the movement from your core, not by kipping.
Keep legs as straight as possible.
Control the descent, don't just drop your legs.
commonMistakes: Bending the knees to fake the range

name: Hanging Knee Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms fully extended.
Engage your core and raise your knees up toward your chest.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower your legs back down with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions, avoiding swinging.
movementPatternDescription: A hanging core exercise raising the knees toward the chest, a more accessible entry point than a full leg raise.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Curl your pelvis under at the top for full ab engagement.
Avoid using momentum to swing your legs up.
Lower with control, don't just drop.
commonMistakes: Swinging the body to generate momentum
Only using the hip flexors without curling the pelvis
